title: senna cearensis
intro: Senna cearensis, also known as the Cassia auriculata, is a plant species belonging to the Fabaceae family, commonly found in tropical and subtropical regions of the world. The plant is used for its medicinal properties, particularly the anthraquinone glycosides, which have laxative and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of senna cearensis include relief from constipation, fever, and inflammation, as well as its potential role in treating digestive disorders and skin conditions. Senna cearensis is traditionally prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or dried extract, and is also used in Ayurvedic and Unani medicine to promote overall well-being.
